turn_order.description: Switching occurs before moves. Move priority determines sequence. Speed determines order among same-priority moves.
turn_order.switching: Occurs before other actions, unless Pursuit is used on the switching Pokemon

speed_calculation.notes: Paralysis divides speed by 4 (changed to 2 in later gens)

accuracy.hit_condition: Random integer 0-255 generated; move hits if random < calculated_accuracy
formula_steps: Start with move base accuracy (out of 255)
Multiply by user accuracy stage multiplier
Multiply by target evasion stage multiplier

brightpowder.notes: Later gens changed this to a multiplier (x0.9 or x0.95)

accuracy.notes: 100% accurate moves have base accuracy 255, but due to the < comparison (not <=), a random value of 255 causes a miss — giving a 1/256 miss chance on otherwise perfect-accuracy moves (same bug as Gen I)

critical_hits.system: Critical hit stage (C) variable, replacing Gen I speed-based system

stage_modifiers.condition: High critical-hit ratio move (e.g. Slash, Karate Chop)
Focus Energy or Dire Hit used

critical_hits.stat_stage_behavior: Critical hits ignore all stat modifiers (including badge bonuses) only if the attacker's offensive stat stage is lower than the target's defensive stat stage

variables.A: Attacker's effective Attack (physical) or Special Attack (special)
variables.D: Target's effective Defense (physical) or Special Defense (special)

stat_caps.overflow_handling: If either offensive or defensive stat exceeds 255, both are divided by 4 (to fit 8-bit register)

randomization.description: Final damage multiplied by random integer from 217 to 255, then divided by 255

stab.description: Same-Type Attack Bonus: if move type matches one of the attacker's types

burn.effect: Attack stat halved before damage calculation
burn.notes: Applied directly to Attack stat, not as a separate damage modifier like in later gens

selfdestruct_explosion.notes: Later gens changed this to doubling base power instead

badge_bonuses.description: In non-link battles, badge stat bonuses multiply the relevant stat by 1.125

type_boost.description: If the attacker's trainer has the badge from the Gym featuring the move's type, move power is multiplied by 1.125

light_ball.notes: Only boosts Special Attack in Gen II (later gens also boost Attack)

thick_club.bug: Offensive stat is capped before item modifier is applied. If Marowak's Attack reaches 512+ before Thick Club, doubling causes overflow past 8-bit register, producing incorrect values

metal_powder.notes: Transformed behavior is Gen II specific

type_boosting_items.notes: Type-boosting held items (e.g. Mystic Water, Charcoal) give x1.1 in Gen II (increased to x1.2 in Gen IV+)

known_bugs.name: Thick Club / Light Ball overflow
known_bugs.description: The offensive stat is capped before the item modifier is applied. If a Marowak's Attack reaches 512+ before Thick Club doubling, the result can overflow the 8-bit register, producing incorrect damage values.

known_bugs.name: Glacier Badge bug
known_bugs.description: Pryce's Glacier Badge Special stat boost has a buggy implementation.

known_bugs.name: 1/256 miss chance
known_bugs.description: Moves with 100% accuracy (255/255) can still miss because the hit check uses strict less-than comparison. A random value of exactly 255 causes the move to miss.
